The model favors Czech Republic at 54% to win, with South Africa offered a 17% chance of victory. This represents a clear but not dominant preference for the European side.

The draw carries a substantial 29% probability in the model's forecast, suggesting the match is unlikely to be one-sided. The gap between Czech Republic's win probability and a draw outcome is narrow enough that the fixture appears competitive, with multiple plausible outcomes clustering in the 17–29 percentage-point range.

Model and sportsbook consensus align closely across all three outcomes, with gaps of 1 percentage point or less on Czech Republic and larger draws. Polymarket pricing shows similarly tight clustering. The broad agreement across sources indicates stable market and model assessment of this matchup.
